chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI Cr. B. A. No. 1301 of 2022 (Osama vs. The State) ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(s) ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------- For hearing of bail application ------------------------------ 18.07.2022 Mr. Shah Imroz Khan, advocate for the applicant Mr. Faheem Hussain Panhawar, D.P.G for the State ------------------------------------------- Irshad Ali Shah J.—It is alleged that applicant with rest of the culprits robbed complainant Tahir Muhammad Yousuf of his mobile phone, for that the present case was registered. The applicant on having been refused bail by learned V-Additional Sessions Judge, Karachi Central has sought for the same from this court by way of instant application u/s 497 Cr.P.C. It is contended by learned counsel for the applicant that the applicant being innocent has been involved in this case falsely by the police by foisting pistol upon him, otherwise he is not named in FIR, therefore, he is entitled to be released on bail on point of further inquiry. Learned DPG for the State has opposed to release of the applicant on bail by contending that on arrest from him has been recovered the crime weapon. Heard arguments and perused the record. Name and description of the applicant are not appearing in the FIR; more so the complainant during course of identification parade was not able to identify him; there is no recovery of robbed article from applicant. In these circumstances, the guilt of the applicant obviously is calling for further inquiry.

Name and description of the applicant are not appearing in the FIR; more so the complainant during course of identification parade was not able to identify him; there is no recovery of robbed article from applicant. In these circumstances, the guilt of the applicant obviously is calling for further inquiry. In view of above, the applicant is admitted to bail, subject to his furnishing surety in sum of Rs.50,000/- (Rupees Fifty Thousand Only) and P.R. bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of learned trial Court. The instant bail application is disposed of accordingly. J U D G E
